Title: The carboxylesterase AtCXE12 converts volatile (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate to (Z)-3-hexenol in Arabidopsis leaves

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydrolysis/conversion of acetate ester GLV to alcohol GLV
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the acetate ester (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate to the alcohol (Z)-3-hexenol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes hydrolysis of exogenous (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate and accumulation of (Z)-3-hexenol in Arabidopsis leaves
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key contributor to (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate esterase activity in leaves; not established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense-related airborne signaling and modulation of defense responses; deters herbivores and attracts natural enemies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaves; transcripts reduced in atcxe12 T-DNA mutants relative to WT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Partial purification and proteomics; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; T-DNA insertion mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling by GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (LC-MS/MS on timsTOF fleX); no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to engineer GLV conversion and defense signaling capacity in plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: atcxe12#1 and atcxe12#2 showed 55% and 47% reduced leaf esterase activity, and reduced (Z)-3-hexenol accumulation after exposure, indicating AtCXE12 is a major but not sole esterase for this reaction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCXE12 was identified by purification/proteomics as a candidate (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate esterase. 2. Recombinant AtCXE12 hydrolyzed (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate to (Z)-3-hexenol in vitro. 3. AtCXE12 showed higher catalytic efficiency toward (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate than AtCXE5. 4. atcxe12 mutants had reduced esterase activity and accumulated less (Z)-3-hexenol after exposure to (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate, demonstrating in planta contribution.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli expression system; Arabidopsis biochemical context
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydrolysis/conversion of acetate ester GLV to alcohol GLV
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the acetate ester (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate to the alcohol (Z)-3-hexenol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recombinant AtCXE5 can hydrolyze (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate in vitro, but its in planta contribution was not supported by mutant assays in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense-related airborne signaling and modulation of defense responses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaves; one mutant had unchanged transcript and another had modest 27% reduction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro substrate assays; Arabidopsis leaves exposed to synthetic (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate in study context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Partial purification and proteomics; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; T-DNA insertion mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (LC-MS/MS on timsTOF fleX); no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for engineering GLV ester turnover, but role in planta requires further validation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtCXE5 co-purified with AtCXE12 and hydrolyzed several (Z)-3-hexenyl esters in vitro. However, atcxe5 mutants did not show significant reduction in leaf esterase activity, so the paper did not establish a clear in planta role
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCXE5 was identified by purification/proteomics as a candidate esterase. 2. Recombinant AtCXE5 hydrolyzed (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate to (Z)-3-hexenol in vitro. 3. It had lower catalytic efficiency for (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate than AtCXE12. 4. atcxe5 mutants showed no significant esterase-activity defect, so in planta contribution remains unresolved.
